funding_references.funder_name: Doctoral Brain+X Seed Grant Program of Tsinghua University
National Natural Science Foundation of China

title: eldBETA SSVEP benchmark dataset for elderly population
description: eldBETA is a large-scale steady-state visually evoked potential (SSVEP) benchmark dataset designed for brain-computer interface (BCI) research in the elderly population. The dataset comprises EEG recordings from 100 healthy older adults (mean age 63.17 years) performing a 9-target SSVEP speller task across 7 sessions, with 64-channel recordings at 1000 Hz sampling rate. This resource provides a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating SSVEP-based BCI algorithms and classifier performance in aging populations.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Synamps2 system (Neuroscan) with 64 channels arranged according to the standard 1005 montage, referenced to Cz, at a sampling rate of 1000 Hz with 50 Hz line frequency notch filtering. Participants performed a 9-target SSVEP speller task with visual stimuli presented at frequencies ranging from 8 to 12 Hz using joint frequency-phase modulation (JFPM). Each trial lasted 5 seconds with visual feedback provided online. Data were organized in BIDS format with GDF file format.
